name: I fell at work a week ago but didn't tell anyone because I thought I was fine—did I ruin my claim?
description: Not necessarily, but you must report it in writing immediately. Delays give insurance companies an excuse to deny your claim by arguing the injury didn't happen

mainEntity.name: I fell at work a week ago but didn't tell anyone because I thought I was fine—did I ruin my claim?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not necessarily, but you must report it in writing immediately. Delays give insurance companies an excuse to deny your claim by arguing the injury didn't happen at work.
acceptedAnswer.description: Failing to report a fall immediately is a common mistake, but it isn't always fatal to your claim. If you can provide a "reasonable excuse" for the delay—such as the pain not manifesting until the next morning—you may still be protected. The biggest risk is that the insurance company will claim the injury happened at home or over the weekend since there is no "official" record on the day it happened. You should report it in writing now, even if it's late, and identify any coworkers who saw you stumble or heard you complain of pain. Contacting a lawyer immediately can help you "rehabilitate" the record and explain the delay to the insurance carrier. Every day you wait now makes the case harder to win.
